title: Science Discovers AT Field
wikitext:
[]: Science Discovers A.T. Field             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Feb 09, 2011 2:57 am I think any fan of Eva will find parts of this Ted Talk... familiar. In fact, if this had been around in '95, we'd all consider it the basis for Anno's "severed arm" preoccupation. The talk is by neurologist V.S. Ramachandran, who details the role mirror neurons can play in "dissolving the barrier between you and other human beings": http://www.ted.com/talks/lang/eng/vs_ramachandran_the_neurons_that_shaped_civilization.html If a real life SEELE were to initiate third impact, this may be their starting point...

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Feb 09, 2011 4:59 am very funny doc, very funny

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Feb 09, 2011 6:16 am How so? This is the AT Field as defined by the series (at least on the human level). If you remove the influence of these neurons, you literally lose the distinction between yourself and another person and begin to associate their feelings as your own. It can't help ya generate fancy ass shields, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Feb 09, 2011 8:13 am Pretty interesting, actually. Thanks for the link.

[]:             Quote Post by KogareruAsuka » Wed Feb 09, 2011 11:36 am Really interesting actually! (with or without the Evangelion associations). Thanks for the link! Will have to test this shit out when i get home on a mate who's missing some bits...

[]:             Quote Post by TaintedHero » Wed Feb 09, 2011 3:09 pm Not exactly an AT, I think he was going more twards instrumentality. AT fields seem like a physical state changer controlled by your mind to warp air and matter into an at sort of field that can be nuetralized by someone elses warping of matter. This presentation was explaining the merging of minds as you just pump a ton of numbing agents into every person.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Feb 09, 2011 3:26 pm Don't any of you guys subscribe to pop(ular) science magazines? My opinion of them isn't exactly high ("popular" part of the description tends to overwhelm substantial "science" often) but mirror neurons are pretty old stuff even in popular media

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Feb 09, 2011 4:02 pm I knew skipping page 103 of the March 2004 issue would come back to haunt me... TaintedHero wrote: Not exactly an AT, I think he was going more twards instrumentality. AT fields seem like a physical state changer controlled by your mind to warp air and matter into an at sort of field that can be nuetralized by someone elses warping of matter. This presentation was explaining the merging of minds as you just pump a ton of numbing agents into every person.

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Wed Feb 09, 2011 4:27 pm Not to mention that this "A.T. Field" is something physical (the skin), whereas the A.T. Field in NGE is more of a metaphysical thing. I agree with TaintedHero as the mirror neuron concept being closer to Instrumentality, but also that the skin could also be kind of an A.T. Field that separates peoples' consciousnesses.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Feb 09, 2011 5:03 pm Oh hey, pseudoscience.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Feb 10, 2011 2:57 pm Xard wrote: Don't any of you guys subscribe to pop(ular) science magazines? My opinion of them isn't exactly high ("popular" part of the description tends to overwhelm substantial "science" often) but mirror neurons are pretty old stuff even in popular media

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Feb 10, 2011 3:11 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: I've heard of them and vaguely new what they did, but I never connected them to AT Fields.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Feb 10, 2011 3:32 pm Xard wrote: well that's because there isn't any real connection Destroying mirror neurons wouldn't make you tang, for starters

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Feb 10, 2011 4:35 pm Is this that same nerve cluster that can create out-of-body experiences when magnetically resonated? I read an article quite some time ago in one of those popular science magazines Xard describes that suggested 'spiritual experiences' are entirely due to that being messed with.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Feb 10, 2011 4:42 pm NemZ wrote: Is this that same nerve cluster that can create out-of-body experiences when magnetically resonated? I read an article quite some time ago in one of those popular science magazines Xard describes that suggested 'spiritual experiences' are entirely due to that being messed with.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Fri Feb 11, 2011 1:02 am AuraTwilight wrote: Oh hey, pseudoscience.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Feb 11, 2011 5:08 pm Well, for one thing, it's just one guy analyzing what some neurons can do, then extrapolating some completely different role and ability without any sort of evidence and, given his religious backround, might be projecting his bias juuuust a tad. It doesn't help that the link you dropped leads to absolutely nothing readable.
